audit_items.proof_trace: The only raw pointer in the file is `data = (const u1 *) s.c_str()` in `b64_encode`; per the C++ standard, `std::string::c_str()` always returns a non-null pointer. All `lookup` tables are `static const char[]` arrays (stack/BSS-resident, never null), and all other memory accesses use `std::string::operator[]` on valid string references. No code path in the file dereferences a pointer that could be null.
